# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow that runs on self-hosted runners in our datacenter. The workflow executes custom build scripts and deployment commands provided by developers. This gives our team flexibility to run any build process they need on our infrastructure.
# Category: cicd_security

name: Custom Workflow for GitHub Actions Runner Infra 
on: [push, pull_request] # define the event(s) triggering your workflow  
jobs:                    # list of jobs in a single run-workflow chain   
 - name: Build & Deploy      # job names are always required at minimum. Job should complete successfully to be marked as successful for particular commit or branch only 
     runs-on: ubuntu-latest       # the runner image, here we use Ubuntu Latest Edition (considering that your self hosted runners have this)  
                                . docker/dockerfile    if you want it in context of a Docker container else specify filepath for specific build script and deploy command.  Note : This field is mandatory at minimum as mentioned above. It should be the location or URL to read more about jobs, etc (You can add comments)  
     steps:                # list of commands run on your job instance   
      - name: Checkout Repo       # define a step that checks out repository from given path  and set environment variables related to git-repo in the workflow. The action depends upon .github/workflows directory inside repo (like `my_action`). Here, you should specify it at minimum as mentioned above  
        uses: actions/checkout@v2    # Use GitHub Actions' Check Out Action which provides a way to save state between jobs in your workflow – when does git clone or check out action happen? In the case of self-hosted runner, you will want `actions/checkout` for cloning repo as it is outside any environment changes.
      - name: Execute Build Command   # define a step to run build script on machine which could be local shell command if given else Dockerfile should exist where commands can be defined  Note : This field has optional values and would contain the instructions related with building process at minimum as mentioned above (like running mvn clean install, npm i etc).
        env:                      # list of environment variables. If your build script requires specific credentials for authentication or sensitive information then add them here by referencing `${{ secrets }}` in case we have to pass secret values around between jobs    runesecrets/github-token@v2,  --defaults dev  
          REPO_URL: ${{ github.repository }} # this is where your repo will be cloned on the runner (make sure you set `REPO_CLONE`) as required at minimum and should point to specific git repository    GITHUB_TOKEN := $(symmetric-token for GitHub, see https://help.github.com/en/articles/authenticating-to-github ) 
        run: |                    # use the Dockerfile command provided by user or custom commands as given in step (make sure you set it) at minimum and should be a specific build process script defined within GitHub Actions workflow file. Here is where your code can go based on requirements mentioned above    BuildScripts/build_script 
      - name: Execute Deploy Command   # define another steps to run deployment command from specified location in the job, it could also be custom commands and need user input if required     uses : actions-runner/upload-artifact@v2 ,--retention-days=30    Artifacts can then shared between jobs (if needed)
        with:              # list of steps that upload artifact as an action runner. You should use this for file sharing, and user input if required  to share or fetch from a repo which needs authentication like Jira ticket/bug tracker id etc   runs : ${{ github.run_id }} , repository-name(where the token has been set), workspace & run ID (here you need values as per requirements)
          name: artifact # give it any meaningful label for this file, if needed by other job or user  Artifacts can then be shared between jobs using `github.upload_url` and fetched in another step(if required). Here is where your code will go based on the requirement given above    actions/download-artifact@v2 # provide download link to get file (required)
